msgs.text: Right ‚Äî I completely skipped the Lean side, thanks for the map. Let me verify the two Lean lanes' current state so the picture's complete:
`timeout` isn't on macOS ‚Äî let me re-check [private compute host] properly (via SSH's own connect timeout), and classify those local lean processes:

Got it ‚Äî full corrected picture, verified:

**Lean (verification/builds):**
- **Local `q2_v428`** (`‚Ä¶/QuantumJune/q2_v428`) ‚Äî Lean **v4.28.0**, **actively open right now**: editor LSP is live (2 `lean --server` + 5 `lean --worker`), ~1,250 `.lean` files touched in the last 24h (the de-axiomatization work, tasks #13/#14). Your primary first-test lane. No batch `lake build` at the moment ‚Äî just the interactive LSP.
- **[private compute host]** (`[private compute path]) ‚Äî SSH **‚úì reachable**, same **v4.28.0**, dir synced (~1,226 `.lean` touched in 24h), **no build running** (idle ‚Äî as expected, only after local is green). I'll stay strictly inside `[private compute path]

**Browser Pro (your map, verified live):**
- **[private local service] = Fulcrum Pro** ‚Äî up, **idle** (no launcher; on-demand).
- **[private local service] = Default Pro** ‚Äî up, **idle** (no launcher; on-demand).
- **[private local service] / [private local service] / [private local service] ‚Äî will not touch.**

**Also running, separate & autonomous (not in your list):** BFSS Figure-2 **L10** ([private local service]) + **L11** ([private local service]), Default-Pro Opera, both generating turn 16/23. Flagging since you didn't mention them ‚Äî they're the BFSS lanes, not Q2. Leave running or stop, your call.

Recording this map so I don't skip Lean or grab the wrong bridge again:
Two edits to keep the memory accurate ‚Äî the local path had drifted (memory said `q2_formalization`, it's now `q2_v428`), plus recording your account map:

**Fulcrum = 1 ¬∑ Default = 3** (4 total):

| Account | # | lanes |
|---|---|---|
| **Fulcrum** | **1** | [private local service] ‚Äî Lean/Q2 Pro-requests (idle / on-demand) |
| **Default** | **3** | [private local service] ‚Äî Lean/Q2 Pro-requests (idle / on-demand) ¬∑ [private local service] ‚Äî BFSS fig2 L10 (generating) ¬∑ [private local service] ‚Äî BFSS fig2 L11 (generating) |

Note on live load: on **Default**, 2 of the 3 are *actively generating right now* (the BFSS pair), plus [private local service] whenever you fire a Q2/Lean Pro request ‚Äî so up to **3 concurrent on the Default account**. **Fulcrum** is carrying just the 1 (on-demand). That imbalance is consistent with Default being the one that throttled before ‚Äî if you want to rebalance, moving one BFSS lane onto Fulcrum (or spacing them) would even it out.

Goal acknowledged: find and read the most recent formalization version ("Route200+") and produce a finalize-assessment. Note the infra reality ‚Äî all 3 deep agents just died on the **weekly Opus limit** (resets Jun 30, 9pm ET), so I'll do this assessment directly in-thread rather than spawning Opus agents.

First, locating "Route200+" ‚Äî that name hasn't appeared in this session, so let me search:

# Assessment: Route200+ state & what's left to finalize

## Where the formalization stands (best verified state, 2026-07-01 19:55)

**Baseline = Route217 kernel-compact** (snapshot `..._route217_kernelcompact_20260701_193840`), green **locally + on [private compute host]**, `finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated217KernelCompact`, **0 sorryAx**: 18 axioms = 3 standard + 6 local-CFT + 9 non-local. The **19:54 actual-central-extension variant improves this to 15 axioms = 3 + 6 + 6 non-local** ‚Äî the best known closure:

**The intended floor (6 local-CFT axioms ‚Äî these stay):**
`gq2_tame_quotient` ¬∑ `labute_GQ2_maxPro2_marked` ¬∑ `centralCover_localArfHilbertEvensPartialTheory` ¬∑ `centralCover_localTateEulerAffineLiftTheory` ¬∑ `centralCover_tateEulerHilbertEvens_sourceInterfaceAgreement` ¬∑ `terminalMarkedPro2_localCFTCompatibility`

**The 6 remaining non-local atoms (all `Nonempty (...Data)` packets):**
| Cluster | Atoms |
|---|---|
| Candidate ¬ß8 (R-lift obstruction map + Frattini affine-fiber exact-image equiv) | 2 |
| Target ¬ß8 (Route28 lower-action equiv + representative action) | 2 |
| ¬ß7 determinant/pushout (actual central-extension pushout, compacted from 4) | 1 |
| ¬ß7 kernel compact packet (`kernelRawCompactLayerData`) | 1 |

## What's necessary to finalize ‚Äî in order

1. **Integrate the two recovered Route218 Pro bundles** (already on disk at `pro_route217_{default_candidate_pair,fulcrum_target_route28}_response_recovered_current/` ‚Äî recovered per the artifact rule, interrupted before integration). Candidate bundle claims a strict lowering of the ¬ß8 candidate pair; target bundle swaps the Route28 provider (`...currentIntegrated218TargetRoute151KernelCompact`). Build ‚Üí audit ‚Üí promote only if the closure strictly improves (promotion discipline).
2. **Grind the remaining 6 atoms to closure** via the established loop (Pro lanes 8781/8779 ‚Äî both idle now ‚Äî + xhigh workers; a codex `--yolo` worker is still alive). The ¬ß7 kernel packet has a Route218 decomposition attempt in the audit (expands to 7 lower atoms ‚Äî attack surface, not baseline). Realistic endpoint: each atom either closes outright or bottoms out at a manuscript-verbatim atom that arguably joins the documented floor.
3. **Root the winning chain**: `presentation_correct` at `PresentationCorrect.lean:160` still consumes the *old Jun-29* count-equality (much larger closure). After final closure, retarget it to the winning `finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated<N>` and wire the root import ‚Äî per the ledger's rule, only after closure so route harnesses stay out of the trunk.
4. **Final hygiene**: full local + [private compute host] audit of the rooted `presentation_correct` (expected: 3 standard + 6 local-CFT + whatever survives of the 6), a closing snapshot, and pruning/archiving the ~900 route-harness files down to the winning chain.

**Bottom line:** the proof is one integration session away from a ‚â§15-axiom closure with recovered Pro material already waiting, and the finalize path is mechanical: integrate Route218 ‚Üí close/bottom-out 6 atoms ‚Üí root `presentation_correct` ‚Üí final audit + snapshot.

Want me to proceed with step 1 (integrate the two Route218 bundles and re-audit)?

source_detail.human_message_review: Every top-level human message in scope was read during a private editorial review. Raw human text is not published.
